targetScope: Junior backend engineering with DevOps-adjacent responsibilities
locationScope: Mexico and remote roles open to Latin America

sources.usageBasis: Manual factual extraction from a publicly accessible employer posting. NotZero stores normalized requirement labels and the source URL, not the job description.

requirements.name: Backend and API design
requirements.context: Six of eight reviewed postings named backend services, APIs, or API integrations. The sample mixes experience levels, so the count signals vocabulary demand rather than a universal entry requirement.

requirements.name: Automated testing
requirements.context: Two of eight postings explicitly named testing. CI/CD postings may also involve tests, but this dataset does not count an implied requirement as an explicit mention.

requirements.name: Containerization
requirements.context: Seven of eight reviewed postings named containers or an orchestration platform. Docker and Kubernetes are not treated as equivalent skills.

requirements.name: Continuous integration and delivery
requirements.context: Six of eight reviewed postings explicitly named CI/CD or a pipeline tool. Tool choice varied across employers.

requirements.name: Cloud platform fundamentals
requirements.context: Seven of eight reviewed postings named at least one cloud platform. The dataset does not claim that one provider is universally preferred.

requirements.name: Infrastructure as code
requirements.context: Four of eight postings named infrastructure as code or a related tool. One mention was presented as desirable rather than mandatory.

requirements.name: Observability
requirements.context: Four of eight reviewed postings named monitoring, logging, or observability. The terms overlap but are not treated as identical.

requirements.name: Relational databases
requirements.context: Three of eight reviewed postings explicitly named relational database work. The curriculum supports exposure, but the prepared project does not demonstrate database implementation.

technicalSources.title: What is Docker?
technicalSources.usageBasis: Official product documentation summarized in NotZero's own words and linked for verification.

technicalSources.title: Continuous integration

technicalSources.title: What is OpenTelemetry?
technicalSources.usageBasis: Official project documentation summarized in NotZero's own words and linked for verification.

methodology: Eight publicly accessible employer postings were manually reviewed on July 18, 2026. No automated scraping or job-description redistribution is used.
Requirement counts record explicit mentions only. Similar terms are normalized conservatively and employer-specific tools remain distinct aliases.
The sample is geographically relevant to Mexico or remote Latin America, but it mixes experience levels and is not a statistical labor-market survey.
Official technical documentation supports tool-behavior relationships. Job frequency alone never proves equivalence between an academic practice and a current tool.
limitations: This small, curated sample can demonstrate the product method but cannot represent every employer or entry-level role.
Postings can change or disappear after the observation date. The dataset version preserves what NotZero reviewed and when.
Several postings target experienced candidates. Their requirements are used as directional current-practice evidence, not as a claim that a graduate must meet every item.
